chipyard.bump_rationale: 661 commits since 1.13.0; Saturn RVV1.0 OoO vector unit, modularisation (separate generator submodules), Chisel 7 experimental, Spike RVV/Zicbom/Zacas bumps, Tacit branch-predictor / instr tracing, SRAM depth/width-list support (PR #2303), BOOM v3 RoCC fix, CTC chip-to-chip bridge work.

selected_path.not_phone_class_reason: A single Rocket RV64GC hart is a boot and integration stepping stone; it is not competitive with a 2028 phone application processor without separate multi-hart, cache, MMU, benchmark, power, thermal, Android, and silicon evidence.

minimum_claim_evidence: documented application CPU topology and frequency/voltage targets
RISC-V application profile and extension matrix with ISA compliance results
cache hierarchy, coherency, cache-maintenance, TLB, and MMU validation
OpenSBI, U-Boot, Linux, and Android boot transcripts on the selected target
CoreMark, STREAM, lmbench, fio, and selected SPEC-like workload results
sustained thermal and power measurement method with raw artifacts
CTS/VTS/userspace evidence before Android compatibility claims

required_platform_blocks.reset: Boot ROM or firmware entry at the e1-chip reset vector
required_platform_blocks.dram: DRAM model sized for OpenSBI plus Linux initramfs smoke

claim_policy.reason: Chipyard/Rocket RV64GC is the selected generated-AP path, but clean repository and CI runs do not carry the generated import manifest or required OpenSBI/Linux/trap/ISA/benchmark transcripts. The Linux-capable CPU claim remains blocked until those artifacts are regenerated and archived; phone-class AP, Android compatibility, production silicon, and release claims remain blocked by phone_2028_target_boundary.
